title: Suspected Rattlesnake Bite — Desert Canyon
briefing: Day hiker struck on the right calf by a suspected rattlesnake roughly 40 minutes ago while scrambling. Two puncture marks visible; swelling and pain progressing up the lower leg. Patient anxious but alert. Trailhead is a 4-hour hike out through desert canyon.
terrain: Narrow desert canyon, 4-hour hike to trailhead; limited helicopter access except at canyon mouth
evacNote: Needs antivenom — keep calm, immobilize limb, track swelling; urgent evac

patient.mechanism: Suspected rattlesnake (pit viper) bite to right calf, ~40 min ago

injuries.notes: Paired puncture marks. Progressive swelling and pain advancing proximally. Leading edge marked with time. No systemic bleeding signs yet.
